text: "My name is Sam.  I've lived here for five years now, and I think I'll be heading on once the pass clears of snow.  There's supposed to be some good begging towns to the south."
speaker: beggar

text: "A beggar gets cash only when others get cash, and nobody's got much money here right now."
speaker: beggar

text: "There's honest work up at the Baron's castle, I hear.  But begging's my business."
speaker: beggar

text: "I remember when people used to stroll around town at night.  Now, the only people out late are the thieves.  And outside of town, man, you don't dare go out there after dark.  The Night Gaunts'll get you for sure."
speaker: beggar

text: "The most dangerous monsters come out at night.  I'd stay inside then if I were you."
speaker: beggar

text: "Nobody's ever lived to tell what the Night Gaunts are.  I sure don't want to find out."
speaker: beggar

text: "Thieves prowl the streets at night.  They don't bother me because I have nothing to steal.  The brigands are worse.  They sure hurt business; no one's getting rich but them."
speaker: beggar

text: "You can get a job cleaning the Baron's stable, I understand.  It's good work if you want to build up your muscles or have a place to spend the night, but you won't get rich.  I'd rather beg, myself."
